title: Simple Crab Linguine

overview.description: A simple Italian-style pasta dish with crab, garlic, chilli, lemon, parsley and olive oil.

ingredients: 1 large handful fresh parsley, finely chopped
method: Bring a large saucepan of salted water to the boil.
Cook the linguine according to the packet instructions until al dente.
While the pasta cooks, heat the olive oil in a large frying pan over low to medium heat.
Add the sliced garlic, tomatoes and chilli.
Cook gently for 1–2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant but not browned.
Reserve approximately 100ml of the pasta cooking water.
Add the linguine to the frying pan.
Add a splash of the reserved pasta water.
Toss thoroughly until the oil and pasta water form a light sauce around the pasta.
Toss gently until just warmed through.
Add the lemon juice and parsley.
Season with black pepper and adjust the salt if necessary.
Add a little more pasta water if needed to loosen the sauce.

learning.did-you-know: Crab meat is delicate and is usually best added near the end of cooking so its flavour and texture are not lost.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to create a simple emulsified pasta sauce using olive oil and starchy pasta water, and how to gently heat delicate seafood.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, elegant seafood pasta that lets a small amount of crab remain the main flavour rather than burying it under a heavy sauce.
chapman-tips: Use the best crab meat you can reasonably obtain.
White crab meat gives delicate sweetness while brown crab meat adds depth.
Don't brown the garlic; burnt garlic will overpower the crab.
Use the pasta water gradually rather than adding all of it at once.
Add the crab right at the end and heat it gently.
The lemon should brighten the dish without making it sour.
Don't add Parmesan; its strong flavour would overpower the crab.
notes.batch-cooking: Better fresh. The crab and pasta are at their best immediately after cooking.
notes.leftovers: Best eaten immediately, although leftovers can be gently reheated with a splash of water.
